{{Infobox Ägyptische Gottheit |NAME = W3:I9-N35-Y5:N35 Aa1:X1-N26-G17-E16
Heb-ef-en-menchet-em-dju-Anubis
''{{unicode|ḥb}}=f-n-{{unicode|mnẖt-m-ḏw-Jnpw}}''
''Fest der Bekleidung von Anubis auf seinem Berg'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = [[Altes Reich (Ägypten)|Altes Reich]] |NAME2-ERWEITERT = [[12. Dynastie|12. Dynastie]] |NAME2 = W3:I9-N35-Y5:N35 Aa1:X1-F12-S29-D21:X1-O34:N35
Heb-ef-en-menchet-Senwosret
''{{unicode|ḥb}}=f-n-{{unicode|mnẖt-S(j)-n-Wsrt}}''
''Fest der Bekleidung von Sesostris II.'' |NAME3-ERWEITERT = '''Festdatum''' |NAME3 = N12:Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME3-TRANSKRIPTION =''{{Unicode|3ḫt}}'' |NAME3-ERKLÄRUNG = ''Erster Tag des Mondes im zweiten Monat der Periode Achet'' |BILD1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 300px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Sesostris-II.-Pyramide in Al-Lahun }} Das '''Menchet-Fest''' (auch '''Tag des Sandholens''') ist seit dem [[Altes Reich (Ägypten)|Alten Reich]] unter [[Niuserre]] belegt und steht in Verbindung der Geburt des [[Horus]]. == Hintergrund == === Altes Reich === Das Menchet-Fest lag nicht auf dem sonst üblichen ersten Tag des [[Neumond]]es, sondern auf dem ersten Tag des [[Neulicht]]es und symbolisierte das ''Bekleiden des [[Mond]]es'' an seinem ersten Sichtbarkeitstag sowie das [[Ritus|Ritual]] vom ''Füllen des [[Horusauge]]es''. === 12. Dynastie === Zu Ehren des vergöttlichten [[Sesostris II.]] änderte sich in der [[12. Dynastie]] unter [[Sesostris III.]] der Festname in '''Bekleidung von Sesostris II.''' mit dem Beinamen ''Tag des Sandholens des Anubis auf seinem Berge in der Pyramidenstadt von Sesostris II., des Seligen''. Die [[Sesostris-II.-Pyramide]] von [[Al-Lahun]] galt mit ihrem Standort als traditionelle Zuweisung zum ''[[Anubis (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Berg des Anubis]]''. Aus dem Tempeltagebuch im 14. Regierungsjahr des Sesostris III. ist die Verkündung im [[Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|Mondkalender]] für den [[Achet|24. Achet II]] am zweiten Mondtag vermerkt. Da im [[Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe]] aus dem 35. Regierungsjahr des Sesostris III. das Menchet-Fest für den zweiten Monat Achet ohne Datumsnennung angesetzt war, lag eine Verkündung des Mondtermins zum Zeitpunkt der Erstellung des damaligen Festkalenders noch nicht vor. Weitere Festtermine ohne Nennung eines Regierungsjahres sind in der 12. Dynastie für zwei aufeinanderfolgende Jahre mit dem 27. Achet II sowie dem 17. Achet II im [[Ägyptischer Kalender|ägyptischen Verwaltungskalender]] belegt und verweisen auf die Ansetzung der Festlichkeiten im Mondkalender. Der gleichnamige Monat [[Menchet]] hatte jedoch keinen direkten Bezug zu diesem Fest, da er nach der Gottheit [[Menchet (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Menchet]] benannt war, die für das Einkleiden der Götter an ihrem Geburtstag zuständig war. == Literatur == * Richard-Anthony Parker: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt''. Chicago Press, Chicago 1950 * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/Wiesbaden 1950 {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Kategorie:Feste und Brauchtum (Altägypten)]]

Chicago Press, Chicago 1950 * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Dynastie === Zu Ehren des vergöttlichten [[Sesostris II.]] änderte sich in der [[12. Dynastie]] unter [[Sesostris III.]] der Festname in '''Bekleidung von Sesostris II.''' mit dem Beinamen ''Tag des Sandholens des Anubis auf seinem Berge in der Pyramidenstadt von Sesostris II., des Seligen''. Die [[Sesostris-II.-Pyramide]] von [[Al-Lahun]] galt mit ihrem Standort als traditionelle Zuweisung zum ''[[Anubis (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Berg des Anubis]]''. Aus dem Tempeltagebuch im 14. Regierungsjahr des Sesostris III. ist die Verkündung im [[Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|Mondkalender]] für den [[Achet|24. Achet II]] am zweiten Mondtag vermerkt. Da im [[Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe]] aus dem 35. Regierungsjahr des [[Amenemhet III.]] das Menchet-Fest für den zweiten Monat Achet ohne Datumsnennung angesetzt war, lag eine Verkündung des Mondtermins zum Zeitpunkt der Erstellung des damaligen Festkalenders noch nicht vor. Weitere Festtermine ohne Nennung eines Regierungsjahres sind in der 12. Dynastie für zwei aufeinanderfolgende Jahre mit dem 27. Achet II sowie dem 17. Achet II im [[Ägyptischer Kalender|ägyptischen Verwaltungskalender]] belegt und verweisen auf die Ansetzung der Festlichkeiten im Mondkalender. Der gleichnamige Monat [[Menchet (Monat)|Menchet]] hatte jedoch keinen direkten Bezug zu diesem Fest, da er nach der Gottheit [[Menchet (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Menchet]] benannt war, die für das Einkleiden der Götter an ihrem Geburtstag zuständig war. == Literatur == * Richard-Anthony Parker: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt''. Chicago Press, Chicago 1950 * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/Wiesbaden 1950 {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Kategorie:Feste und Brauchtum (Altägypten)]]

Heb-ef-en-menchet-em-dju-Anubis
''{{unicode|ḥb}}=f-n-{{unicode|mnẖt-m-ḏw-Jnp.w}}''
''Fest der Bekleidung von Anubis auf seinem Berg'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = [[Altes Reich (Ägypten)|Altes Reich]] |NAME2-ERWEITERT = [[12. Dynastie|12. Dynastie]] |NAME2 = W3:I9-N35-Y5:N35 Aa1:X1-F12-S29-D21:X1-O34:N35
Heb-ef-en-menchet-Senwosret
''{{unicode|ḥb}}=f-n-{{unicode|mnẖt-S(j)-n-Wsrt}}''
''Fest der Bekleidung von Sesostris II.'' |NAME3 = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2
Heb-chenep-scha
''{{unicode|ḥb-ẖnp}}-š[[Datei:U-A725 LATIN SMALL LETTER EGYPTOLOGICAL AIN.gif|7px]]''
''Fest des Sandschiebens / Sandholens''
''Fest des Sandausschüttens'' |NAME4-ERWEITERT = '''Festdatum''' |NAME4 = N12:Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME4-TRANSKRIPTION =''{{Unicode|3ḫt}}'' |NAME4-ERKLÄRUNG = ''Erster Tag des Mondes im zweiten Monat der Periode Achet'' |BILD1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 300px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Sesostris-II.-Pyramide in Al-Lahun }} Das '''Menchet-Fest''' (auch '''Chenep-scha''') ist seit dem [[Altes Reich (Ägypten)|Alten Reich]] unter [[Niuserre]] belegt. Es steht in Verbindung mit der Geburt des [[Horus]] und der im [[Mittleres Reich|Mittleren Reich]] in [[Theben (Ägypten)|Theben]] bezeugten Göttin [[Wosret]], die als Schützerin des jugendlichen Horus verehrt wurde. == Hintergrund == === Altes Reich === Das Menchet-Fest lag nicht auf dem sonst üblichen ersten Tag des [[Neumond]]es, sondern auf dem ersten Tag des [[Neulicht]]es und symbolisierte das ''Bekleiden des [[Mond]]es'' an seinem ersten Sichtbarkeitstag sowie das [[Ritus|Ritual]] vom ''Füllen des [[Horusauge]]s''. === 12. Dynastie === Zu Ehren des vergöttlichten [[Sesostris II.]] wurde in der [[12. Dynastie]] unter [[Sesostris III.]] neben dem alten Festnamen ''Chenep-scha'' auch die Bezeichnung '''Bekleidung von Sesostris II.''' mit dem Beinamen ''Tag des Sandholens des Anubis auf seinem Berge in der Pyramidenstadt von Sesostris II., des Seligen'' geführt. Die [[Sesostris-II.-Pyramide]] von [[Al-Lahun]] galt mit ihrem Standort als traditionelle Zuweisung zum ''[[Anubis (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Berg des Anubis]]''. Aus dem Tempeltagebuch im 14. Regierungsjahr des Sesostris III. ist die Verkündung im [[Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|Mondkalender]] für den [[Achet|24. Achet II]] am zweiten Mondtag vermerkt. Da im [[Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe]] aus dem 35. Regierungsjahr des [[Amenemhet III.]] das Menchet-Fest für den zweiten Monat Achet ohne Datumsnennung angesetzt war, lag eine Verkündung des Mondtermins zum Zeitpunkt der Erstellung des damaligen Festkalenders noch nicht vor. Weitere Festtermine ohne Nennung eines Regierungsjahres sind in der 12. Dynastie für zwei aufeinanderfolgende Jahre mit dem 27. Achet II sowie dem 17. Achet II im [[Ägyptischer Kalender|ägyptischen Verwaltungskalender]] belegt und verweisen auf die Ansetzung der Festlichkeiten im Mondkalender. Der gleichnamige Monat [[Menchet (Monat)|Menchet]] hatte jedoch keinen direkten Bezug zu diesem Fest, da er nach der Gottheit [[Menchet (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Menchet]] benannt war, die für das Einkleiden der Götter an ihrem Geburtstag zuständig war. == Literatur == * Rainer Hannig: ''Großes Handwörterbuch Ägyptisch-Deutsch : (2800 - 950 v. Chr.)''. von Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. S. 649. * Rolf Krauss: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'', Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}} * Richard-Anthony Parker: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt''. Chicago Press, Chicago 1950 * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/Wiesbaden 1950 {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Kategorie:Feste und Brauchtum (Altägypten)]]

Es steht in direkter Verbindung zum Fest [[Chenep-scha]]. == Hintergrund == === Altes Reich === Das Menchet-Fest lag nicht auf dem sonst üblichen ersten Tag des [[Neumond]]es, sondern folgte direkt nach dem Fest Chenep-scha am dritten Tag symbolisierte das ''Bekleiden des [[Mond]]es''. === 12. Dynastie]] unter [[Sesostris III.]] auch die Bezeichnung '''Bekleidung von Sesostris II.''' nach dem ''Tag des Sandholens des Anubis auf seinem Berge in der Pyramidenstadt von Sesostris II., des Seligen'' geführt. Die [[Sesostris-II.-Pyramide]] von [[Al-Lahun]] galt mit ihrem Standort als traditionelle Zuweisung zum ''[[Anubis (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Berg des Anubis]]''. [[Ludwig Borchardt]] nannte als Festtermine zwei aufeinanderfolgende Jahre für den [[Achet|27. Achet II]] sowie dem 17. Achet II. Zwischenzeitlich konnten die Datierungen für das 36. und 37. Regierungsjahr des [[Amenemhet III.]] zugeordnet werden. Der gleichnamige Monat [[Menchet (Monat)|Menchet]] hatte jedoch keinen direkten Bezug zu diesem Fest, da er nach der Gottheit [[Menchet (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Menchet]] benannt war, die für das Einkleiden der Götter an ihrem Geburtstag zuständig war. == Literatur == * Rolf Krauss: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'', Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}} * Richard-Anthony Parker: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt''. Dynastie === Zu Ehren des vergöttlichten [[Sesostris II.]] wurde in der [[12. Dynastie]] unter [[Sesostris III.]] auch die Bezeichnung '''Bekleidung von Sesostris II.''' nach dem ''Tag des Sandholens des Anubis auf seinem Berge in der Pyramidenstadt von Sesostris II., des Seligen'' geführt. Die [[Sesostris-II.-Pyramide]] von [[Al-Lahun]] galt mit ihrem Standort als traditionelle Zuweisung zum ''[[Anubis (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Berg des Anubis]]''. [[Ludwig Borchardt]] nannte als Festtermine zwei aufeinanderfolgende Jahre für den [[Achet|27. Achet II]] sowie dem 17. Achet II. Zwischenzeitlich konnten die Datierungen für das 36. und 37. Regierungsjahr des [[Amenemhet III.]] zugeordnet werden. Der gleichnamige Monat [[Menchet (Monat)|Menchet]] hatte jedoch keinen direkten Bezug zu diesem Fest, da er nach der Gottheit [[Menchet (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Menchet]] benannt war, die für das Einkleiden der Götter an ihrem Geburtstag zuständig war. == Literatur == * Rolf Krauss: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'', Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}} * Richard-Anthony Parker: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt''. Chicago Press, Chicago 1950 * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/Wiesbaden 1950 {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Kategorie:Feste und Brauchtum (Altägypten)]] [[Kategorie:Festkalender des Monats Achet II]]

Der gleichnamige Monat [[Menchet (Monat)|Menchet]] hatte jedoch keinen direkten Bezug zu diesem Fest, da er nach der Gottheit [[Menchet (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Menchet]] benannt war, die für das Einkleiden der Götter an ihrem Geburtstag zuständig war. == Literatur == * [[Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer ägyptologische Beiträge.'' Bd. 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * [[Richard Anthony Parker|Richard A. Parker]]: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Bd. 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse'' 1950, 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur u. a., Mainz u. a. 1950. {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Kategorie:Feste und Brauchtum (Altägypten)]] [[Kategorie:Festkalender des Monats Achet II]]

''ḥb=f-n-{{unicode|mnḫt-m-ḏw-Jnp.w}}''
''Fest der Bekleidung von Anubis auf seinem Berg'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = [[Altes Reich (Ägypten)|Altes Reich]] |NAME2-ERWEITERT = [[12. Dynastie|12. Dynastie]] |NAME2 = W3:I9-N35-Y5:N35 Aa1:X1-F12-S29-D21:X1-O34:N35
Heb-ef-en-menchet-Senwosret
''ḥb=f-n-{{unicode|mnḫt-S(j)-n-Wsrt}}''
''Fest der Bekleidung von Sesostris II.'' |NAME4-ERWEITERT = '''Festdatum''' |NAME4 = N12:Z1*Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME4-TRANSKRIPTION =''{{Unicode|3ḫt}}'' |NAME4-ERKLÄRUNG = ''Dritter Tag des Mondes im zweiten Monat der Periode Achet'' |BILD1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 300px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Sesostris-II.-Pyramide in Al-Lahun }} Das '''Menchet-Fest''' ist seit dem [[Altes Reich (Ägypten)|Alten Reich]] unter [[Niuserre]] belegt. Es steht in direkter Verbindung zum Fest [[Chenep-scha]]. == Hintergrund == === Altes Reich === Das Menchet-Fest fand am dritten Tag nach [[Neumond]] im Anschluss an das Fest Chenep-scha statt und symbolisierte das ''Bekleiden des [[Mond]]es''. === 12. Dynastie === Zu Ehren des vergöttlichten [[Sesostris II.]] wurde in der [[12. Dynastie]] unter [[Sesostris III.]] auch die Bezeichnung '''Bekleidung von Sesostris II.''' nach dem ''Tag des Sandholens des Anubis auf seinem Berge in der Pyramidenstadt von Sesostris II., des Seligen'' geführt. Die [[Sesostris-II.-Pyramide]] von [[Al-Lahun]] galt mit ihrem Standort als traditionelle Zuweisung zum ''[[Anubis (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Berg des Anubis]]''. In den [[Ludwig Borchardt]] vorliegenden [[Papyrus|Papyri]] ist der Festtermin für zwei Jahre angegeben; der [[Achet|27. Achet II]] sowie für das Folgejahr der 17. Achet II. Zwischenzeitlich konnten die Datierungen dem 36. und 37. Regierungsjahr des [[Amenemhet III.]] zugeordnet werden. Der gleichnamige Monat [[Menchet (Monat)|Menchet]] hatte jedoch keinen direkten Bezug zu diesem Fest, da er nach der Gottheit [[Menchet (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Menchet]] benannt war, die für das Einkleiden der Götter an ihrem Geburtstag zuständig war. == Literatur == * [[Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer ägyptologische Beiträge.'' Bd. 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * [[Richard Anthony Parker|Richard A. Parker]]: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Bd. 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse'' 1950, 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur u. a., Mainz u. a. 1950. {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Kategorie:Feste und Brauchtum (Altägypten)]] [[Kategorie:Festkalender des Monats Achet II]]

''Festival of the Clothing of Anubis on His Mountain'' |NAME-EXTENDED = {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)}} |NAME2-EXTENDED = {{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}} |NAME2 = W3:I9-N35-Y5:N35 Aa1:X1-F12-S29-D21:X1-O34:N35
Heb-ef-en-menchet-Senwosret
''ḥb=f-n-mnḫt-S(j)-n-Wsrt''
''Festival of the Clothing of {{ill|Senusret II|de|Sesostris II}}'' |NAME4-EXTENDED = '''Festival Date''' |NAME4 = N12:Z1*Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME4-TRANSLITERATION =''3ḫt'' |NAME4-EXPLANATION = ''Third Day of the Lunar Month in the Second Month of the {{ill|Akhet|de|Achet}} Season'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-CAPTION = {{ill|Pyramid of Senusret II|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide}} in {{ill|El-Lahun|de|Al-Lahun}} }} The **Menchet Festival** has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)}}, during the reign of {{ill|Nyuserre Ini|de|Niuserre}}. It is directly related to the festival of **{{ill|Chenep-scha|de|Chenep-scha}}**. ## **Background** ### **Old Kingdom** The **Menchet Festival** took place on the **third day after the New Moon**, following the **Chenep-scha Festival**, and symbolized the **"clothing of the Moon."** ### **Twelfth Dynasty** In honor of the deified **{{ill|Senusret II|de|Sesostris II}}**, during the reign of **{{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III}}**, the festival was also referred to as **"The Clothing of Senusret II."** It was celebrated **after the "Day of Fetching Sand for Anubis on His Mountain"** in the **Pyramid City of Senusret II, the Blessed One**. The **{{ill|Pyramid of Senusret II|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide}}** at **{{ill|El-Lahun|de|Al-Lahun}}** was traditionally associated with the **"Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis}}."** In the papyri examined by **{{ill|Ludwig Borchardt|de|Ludwig Borchardt}}**, festival dates are recorded for **two different years**: - **27th of Akhet II** - **17th of Akhet II** in the following year. These have since been correlated to the **36th and 37th regnal years of {{ill|Amenemhat III|de|Amenemhet III}}.** However, the **similarly named month** **{{ill|Menchet (month)|de|Menchet (Monat)}}** had **no direct connection to this festival**. The month was named after the **goddess {{ill|Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|de|Menchet (ägyptische Mythologie)}}**, who was responsible for **clothing the gods on their birthdays**. ## **Further Reading** * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss}} |title=Sothic and Lunar Dates: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt |series=Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge |volume=20 |publisher=Gerstenberg |location=Hildesheim |year=1985 |isbn=3-8067-8086-X}} * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker}} |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |year=1950}} * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott}} |editor={{ill|Academy of Sciences and Literature|de|Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur}} |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |year=1950}} {{Navigation Ancient Egyptian Calendar}} [[Category:Religious Festivals of Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Festival Calendar of Month Akhet II]] [[de:Menchet-Fest]]

''Festival of the Clothing of {{ill|Senusret II|de|Sesostris II}}'' |NAME4-EXTENDED = '''Festival Date''' |NAME4 = N12:Z1*Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME4-TRANSLITERATION =''3ḫt'' |NAME4-EXPLANATION = ''Third Day of the Lunar Month in the Second Month of the {{ill|Akhet|de|Achet}} Season'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-CAPTION = {{ill|Pyramid of Senusret II|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide}} in {{ill|El-Lahun|de|Al-Lahun}} }} The Menchet Festival has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)}}, during the reign of {{ill|Nyuserre Ini|de|Niuserre}}. It is directly related to the festival of {{ill|Chenep-scha|de|Chenep-scha}}. == Background == === Old Kingdom === The Menchet Festival took place on the third day after the New Moon, following the Chenep-scha Festival, and symbolized the "clothing of the Moon." == Twelfth Dynasty == In honor of the deified {{ill|Senusret II|de|Sesostris II}}, during the reign of {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III}}, the festival was also referred to as "The Clothing of Senusret II." It was celebrated after the "Day of Fetching Sand for Anubis on His Mountain" in the Pyramid City of Senusret II, the Blessed One. The {{ill|Pyramid of Senusret II|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide}} at {{ill|El-Lahun|de|Al-Lahun}} was traditionally associated with the "Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis}}." In the papyri examined by {{ill|Ludwig Borchardt|de|Ludwig Borchardt}}, festival dates are recorded for two different years: - 27th of Akhet II - 17th of Akhet II in the following year. These have since been correlated to the 36th and 37th regnal years of {{ill|Amenemhat III|de|Amenemhet III}}. However, the similarly named month {{ill|Menchet (month)|de|Menchet (Monat)}} had no direct connection to this festival. These have since been correlated to the 36th and 37th regnal years of {{ill|Amenemhat III|de|Amenemhet III}}. However, the similarly named month {{ill|Menchet (month)|de|Menchet (Monat)}} had no direct connection to this festival. The month was named after the goddess {{ill|Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|de|Menchet (ägyptische Mythologie)}}, who was responsible for clothing the gods on their birthdays. == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss}} |title=Sothic and Lunar Dates: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt |series=Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge |volume=20 |publisher=Gerstenberg |location=Hildesheim |year=1985 |isbn=3-8067-8086-X}} * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker}} |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |year=1950}} * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott}} |editor={{ill|Academy of Sciences and Literature|de|Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur}} |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |year=1950}} [[Category:Religious Festivals of Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Festival Calendar of Month Akhet II]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[de:Menchet-Fest]] {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}}

''Festival of the Clothing of {{ill|Senusret II|de|Sesostris II|12=draft}}'' |NAME4-EXTENDED = '''Festival Date''' |NAME4 = N12:Z1*Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME4-TRANSLITERATION =''3ḫt'' |NAME4-EXPLANATION = ''Third Day of the Lunar Month in the Second Month of the {{ill|Akhet|de|Achet|12=draft}} Season'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-CAPTION = {{ill|Pyramid of Senusret II|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|12=draft}} in {{ill|El-Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|12=draft}} }} The Menchet Festival has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|12=draft}}, during the reign of {{ill|Nyuserre Ini|de|Niuserre|12=draft}}. It is directly related to the festival of {{ill|Chenep-scha|de|Chenep-scha|12=draft}}. == Background == === Old Kingdom === The Menchet Festival took place on the third day after the New Moon, following the Chenep-scha Festival, and symbolized the "clothing of the Moon." == Twelfth Dynasty == In honor of the deified {{ill|Senusret II|de|Sesostris II|12=draft}}, during the reign of {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III|12=draft}}, the festival was also referred to as "The Clothing of Senusret II." It was celebrated after the "Day of Fetching Sand for Anubis on His Mountain" in the Pyramid City of Senusret II, the Blessed One. The {{ill|Pyramid of Senusret II|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|12=draft}} at {{ill|El-Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|12=draft}} was traditionally associated with the "Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|12=draft}}." In the papyri examined by {{ill|Ludwig Borchardt|de|Ludwig Borchardt|12=draft}}, festival dates are recorded for two different years: - 27th of Akhet II - 17th of Akhet II in the following year. These have since been correlated to the 36th and 37th regnal years of {{ill|Amenemhat III|de|Amenemhet III|12=draft}}. However, the similarly named month {{ill|Menchet (month)|de|Menchet (Monat)|12=draft}} had no direct connection to this festival. The month was named after the goddess {{ill|Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|de|Menchet (ägyptische Mythologie)|12=draft}}, who was responsible for clothing the gods on their birthdays. == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=draft}} |title=Sothic and Lunar Dates: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt |series=Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge |volume=20 |publisher=Gerstenberg |location=Hildesheim |year=1985 |isbn=3-8067-8086-X}} * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|12=draft}} |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |year=1950}} * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=draft}} |editor={{ill|Academy of Sciences and Literature|de|Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|12=draft}} |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |year=1950}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Religious Festivals of Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Festival Calendar of Month Akhet II]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Menchet Festival]] [[de:Menchet-Fest]]

Heb-ef-en-menchet-Senwosret
''ḥb=f-n-mnḫt-S(j)-n-Wsrt''
''Festival of the Clothing of {{ill|Senusret II|de|Sesostris II|12=draft}}'' |NAME4-EXTENDED = '''Festival Date''' |NAME4 = N12:Z1*Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME4-TRANSLITERATION =''3ḫt'' |NAME4-EXPLANATION = ''Third Day of the Lunar Month in the Second Month of the {{ill|Akhet|de|Achet|12=draft}} Season'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-CAPTION = {{ill|Pyramid of Senusret II|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|12=draft}} in {{ill|El-Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|12=draft}} }} The Menchet Festival has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|12=draft}}, during the reign of {{ill|Nyuserre Ini|de|Niuserre|12=draft}}. It is directly related to the festival of {{ill|Chenep-scha|de|Chenep-scha|12=draft}}. == Background == === Old Kingdom === The Menchet Festival took place on the third day after the New Moon, following the Chenep-scha Festival, and symbolized the "clothing of the Moon." == Twelfth Dynasty == In honor of the deified {{ill|Senusret II|de|Sesostris II|12=draft}}, during the reign of {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III|12=draft}}, the festival was also referred to as "The Clothing of Senusret II." It was celebrated after the "Day of Fetching Sand for Anubis on His Mountain" in the Pyramid City of Senusret II, the Blessed One. The {{ill|Pyramid of Senusret II|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|12=draft}} at {{ill|El-Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|12=draft}} was traditionally associated with the "Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|12=draft}}." In the papyri examined by {{ill|Ludwig Borchardt|de|Ludwig Borchardt|12=draft}}, festival dates are recorded for two different years: - 27th of Akhet II - 17th of Akhet II in the following year. These have since been correlated to the 36th and 37th regnal years of {{ill|Amenemhat III|de|Amenemhet III|12=draft}}. However, the similarly named month {{ill|Menchet (month)|de|Menchet (Monat)|12=draft}} had no direct connection to this festival. The month was named after the goddess {{ill|Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|de|Menchet (ägyptische Mythologie)|12=draft}}, who was responsible for clothing the gods on their birthdays. == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=draft}} |title=Sothic and Lunar Dates: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt |series=Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge |volume=20 |publisher=Gerstenberg |location=Hildesheim |year=1985 |isbn=3-8067-8086-X}} * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|12=draft}} |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |year=1950}} * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=draft}} |editor={{ill|Academy of Sciences and Literature|de|Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|12=draft}} |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |year=1950}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Religious Festivals of Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Festival Calendar of Month Akhet II]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Menchet Festival]] [[de:Menchet-Fest]] [[Category:ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Menchet Festival]][[Category:qq]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}}

''Festival of the Clothing of {{ill|Senusret II|de|Sesostris II|12=draft}}'' |NAME4-EXTENDED = '''Festival Date''' |NAME4 = N12:Z1*Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME4-TRANSLITERATION =''3ḫt'' |NAME4-EXPLANATION = ''Third Day of the Lunar Month in the Second Month of the {{ill|Akhet|de|Achet|12=draft}} Season'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-CAPTION = {{ill|Pyramid of Senusret II|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|12=draft}} in {{ill|El-Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|12=draft}} }} The Menchet Festival has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|12=draft}}, during the reign of {{ill|Nyuserre Ini|de|Niuserre|12=draft}}. It is directly related to the festival of {{ill|Chenep-scha|de|Chenep-scha|12=draft}}. == Background == === Old Kingdom === The Menchet Festival took place on the third day after the New Moon, following the Chenep-scha Festival, and symbolized the "clothing of the Moon." == Twelfth Dynasty == In honor of the deified {{ill|Senusret II|de|Sesostris II|12=draft}}, during the reign of {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III|12=draft}}, the festival was also referred to as "The Clothing of Senusret II." It was celebrated after the "Day of Fetching Sand for Anubis on His Mountain" in the Pyramid City of Senusret II, the Blessed One. The {{ill|Pyramid of Senusret II|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|12=draft}} at {{ill|El-Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|12=draft}} was traditionally associated with the "Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|12=draft}}." In the papyri examined by {{ill|Ludwig Borchardt|de|Ludwig Borchardt|12=draft}}, festival dates are recorded for two different years: - 27th of Akhet II - 17th of Akhet II in the following year. These have since been correlated to the 36th and 37th regnal years of {{ill|Amenemhat III|de|Amenemhet III|12=draft}}. However, the similarly named month {{ill|Menchet (month)|de|Menchet (Monat)|12=draft}} had no direct connection to this festival. The month was named after the goddess {{ill|Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|de|Menchet (ägyptische Mythologie)|12=draft}}, who was responsible for clothing the gods on their birthdays. == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=draft}} |title=Sothic and Lunar Dates: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt |series=Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge |volume=20 |publisher=Gerstenberg |location=Hildesheim |year=1985 |isbn=3-8067-8086-X}} * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|12=draft}} |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |year=1950}} * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=draft}} |editor={{ill|Academy of Sciences and Literature|de|Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|12=draft}} |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |year=1950}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Religious Festivals of Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Festival Calendar of Month Akhet II]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Menchet Festival]] [[de:Menchet-Fest]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Menchet Festival]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Menchet Festival]][[Category:qq]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}}

The month was named after the goddess {{ill|Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|de|Menchet (ägyptische Mythologie)|12=draft|12=draft}}, who was responsible for clothing the gods on their birthdays. == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=draft|12=draft}} |title=Sothic and Lunar Dates: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt |series=Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge |volume=20 |publisher=Gerstenberg |location=Hildesheim |year=1985 |isbn=3-8067-8086-X}} * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|12=draft|12=draft}} |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |year=1950}} * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=draft|12=draft}} |editor={{ill|Academy of Sciences and Literature|de|Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|12=draft|12=draft}} |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |year=1950}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{draft categories| }} {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Religious Festivals of Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Festival Calendar of Month Akhet II]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] }} [[sn:Menchet Festival]] [[de:Menchet-Fest]]

Heb-ef-en-menchet-Senwosret
''ḥb=f-n-mnḫt-S(j)-n-Wsrt''
''Festival of the Clothing of {{ill|Senusret II|de|Sesostris II|12=draft|12=draft}}'' |NAME4-EXTENDED = '''Festival Date''' |NAME4 = N12:Z1*Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME4-TRANSLITERATION =''3ḫt'' |NAME4-EXPLANATION = ''Third Day of the Lunar Month in the Second Month of the {{ill|Akhet|de|Achet|12=draft|12=draft}} Season'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-CAPTION = {{ill|Pyramid of Senusret II|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|El-Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|12=draft|12=draft}} }} The Menchet Festival has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|12=draft|12=draft}}, during the reign of {{ill|Nyuserre Ini|de|Niuserre|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is directly related to the festival of {{ill|Chenep-scha|de|Chenep-scha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Background == === Old Kingdom === The Menchet Festival took place on the third day after the New Moon, following the Chenep-scha Festival, and symbolized the "clothing of the Moon." == Twelfth Dynasty == In honor of the deified {{ill|Senusret II|de|Sesostris II|12=draft|12=draft}}, during the reign of {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III|12=draft|12=draft}}, the festival was also referred to as "The Clothing of Senusret II." It was celebrated after the "Day of Fetching Sand for Anubis on His Mountain" in the Pyramid City of Senusret II, the Blessed One. The {{ill|Pyramid of Senusret II|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|El-Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|12=draft|12=draft}} was traditionally associated with the "Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|12=draft|12=draft}}." In the papyri examined by {{ill|Ludwig Borchardt|de|Ludwig Borchardt|12=draft|12=draft}}, festival dates are recorded for two different years: - 27th of Akhet II - 17th of Akhet II in the following year. These have since been correlated to the 36th and 37th regnal years of {{ill|Amenemhat III|de|Amenemhet III|12=draft|12=draft}}. However, the similarly named month {{ill|Menchet (month)|de|Menchet (Monat)|12=draft|12=draft}} had no direct connection to this festival. The month was named after the goddess {{ill|Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|de|Menchet (ägyptische Mythologie)|12=draft|12=draft}}, who was responsible for clothing the gods on their birthdays. == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=draft|12=draft}} |title=Sothic and Lunar Dates: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt |series=Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge |volume=20 |publisher=Gerstenberg |location=Hildesheim |year=1985 |isbn=3-8067-8086-X}} * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|12=draft|12=draft}} |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |year=1950}} * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=draft|12=draft}} |editor={{ill|Academy of Sciences and Literature|de|Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|12=draft|12=draft}} |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |year=1950}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{draft categories|}} {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Religious Festivals of Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Festival Calendar of Month Akhet II]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] }} [[sn:Menchet Festival]] [[de:Menchet-Fest]]

''Festival of the Clothing of Anubis on His Mountain'' |NAME-EXTENDED = {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|12=draft|12=draft}} |NAME2-EXTENDED = {{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|12=draft|12=draft}} |NAME2 = W3:I9-N35-Y5:N35 Aa1:X1-F12-S29-D21:X1-O34:N35
Heb-ef-en-menchet-Senwosret
''ḥb=f-n-mnḫt-S(j)-n-Wsrt''
''Festival of the Clothing of {{ill|Senusret II|de|Sesostris II|12=draft|12=draft}}'' |NAME4-EXTENDED = '''Festival Date''' |NAME4 = N12:Z1*Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME4-TRANSLITERATION =''3ḫt'' |NAME4-EXPLANATION = ''Third Day of the Lunar Month in the Second Month of the {{ill|Akhet|de|Achet|12=draft|12=draft}} Season'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-CAPTION = {{ill|Pyramid of Senusret II|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|El-Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|12=draft|12=draft}} }} The Menchet Festival has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|12=draft|12=draft}}, during the reign of {{ill|Nyuserre Ini|de|Niuserre|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is directly related to the festival of {{ill|Chenep-scha|de|Chenep-scha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Background == === Old Kingdom === The Menchet Festival took place on the third day after the New Moon, following the Chenep-scha Festival, and symbolized the "clothing of the Moon." == Twelfth Dynasty == In honor of the deified {{ill|Senusret II|de|Sesostris II|12=draft|12=draft}}, during the reign of {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III|12=draft|12=draft}}, the festival was also referred to as "The Clothing of Senusret II." It was celebrated after the "Day of Fetching Sand for Anubis on His Mountain" in the Pyramid City of Senusret II, the Blessed One. The {{ill|Pyramid of Senusret II|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|El-Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|12=draft|12=draft}} was traditionally associated with the "Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|12=draft|12=draft}}." In the papyri examined by {{ill|Ludwig Borchardt|de|Ludwig Borchardt|12=draft|12=draft}}, festival dates are recorded for two different years: - 27th of Akhet II - 17th of Akhet II in the following year. These have since been correlated to the 36th and 37th regnal years of {{ill|Amenemhat III|de|Amenemhet III|12=draft|12=draft}}. However, the similarly named month {{ill|Menchet (month)|de|Menchet (Monat)|12=draft|12=draft}} had no direct connection to this festival. The month was named after the goddess {{ill|Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|de|Menchet (ägyptische Mythologie)|12=draft|12=draft}}, who was responsible for clothing the gods on their birthdays. == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=draft|12=draft}} |title=Sothic and Lunar Dates: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt |series=Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge |volume=20 |publisher=Gerstenberg |location=Hildesheim |year=1985 |isbn=3-8067-8086-X}} * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|12=draft|12=draft}} |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |year=1950}} * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=draft|12=draft}} |editor={{ill|Academy of Sciences and Literature|de|Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|12=draft|12=draft}} |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |year=1950}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Religious Festivals of Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Festival Calendar of Month Akhet II]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] }} [[sn:Menchet Festival]] [[de:Menchet-Fest]]

It is directly related to the festival of {{ill|Chenep-scha|de|Chenep-scha|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Background == === Old Kingdom === The Menchet Festival took place on the third day after the New Moon, following the Chenep-scha Festival, and symbolized the "clothing of the Moon." == Twelfth Dynasty == In honor of the deified {{ill|Senusret II|de|Sesostris II|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, during the reign of {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, the festival was also referred to as "The Clothing of Senusret II." It was celebrated after the "Day of Fetching Sand for Anubis on His Mountain" in the Pyramid City of Senusret II, the Blessed One. The {{ill|Pyramid of Senusret II|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|El-Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} was traditionally associated with the "Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}." However, the similarly named month {{ill|Menchet (month)|de|Menchet (Monat)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} had no direct connection to this festival. These have since been correlated to the 36th and 37th regnal years of {{ill|Amenemhat III|de|Amenemhet III|12=draft}}. However, the similarly named month {{ill|Menchet (month)|de|Menchet (Monat)|12=draft}} had no direct connection to this festival. The month was named after the goddess {{ill|Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|de|Menchet (ägyptische Mythologie)|12=draft}}, who was responsible for clothing the gods on their birthdays. == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=draft}} |title=Sothic and Lunar Dates: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt |series=Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge |volume=20 |publisher=Gerstenberg |location=Hildesheim |year=1985 |isbn=3-8067-8086-X}} * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|12=draft}} |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |year=1950}} * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=draft}} |editor={{ill|Academy of Sciences and Literature|de|Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|12=draft}} |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |year=1950}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{translated page|de|Menchet-Fest|version=201349399|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Religious Festivals of Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Festival Calendar of Month Akhet II]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] }} [[sn:Menchet Festival]] [[de:Menchet-Fest]]

These have since been correlated to the 36th and 37th regnal years of {{ill|Amenemhat III|de|Amenemhet III|qq=draft}}. The month was named after the goddess {{ill|Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|de|Menchet (ägyptische Mythologie)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|qq=}}, who was responsible for clothing the gods on their birthdays. == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}} |title=Sothic and Lunar Dates: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt |series=Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge |volume=20 |publisher=Gerstenberg |location=Hildesheim |year=1985 |isbn=3-8067-8086-X}} * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Richard Anthony Parker|qq=}} |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |year=1950}} * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}} |editor={{ill|Academy of Sciences and Literature|de|Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Academy of Sciences and Literature|qq=}} |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |year=1950}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} [[Category:pages that had Egyptian Calendar Navbar]] {{translated page|de|Menchet-Fest|version=201349399|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Religious Festivals of Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Festival Calendar of Month Akhet II]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Menchet Festival]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[sn:Menchet Festival]] [[de:Menchet-Fest]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}}

''ḥb=f-n-mnḫt-S(j)-n-Wsrt''
''Festival of the Clothing of {{ill|Senusret II|de|Sesostris II|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senusret II|qq=}}'' |NAME4-EXTENDED = '''Festival Date''' |NAME4 = N12:Z1*Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME4-TRANSLITERATION =''3ḫt'' |NAME4-EXPLANATION = ''Third Day of the Lunar Month in the Second Month of the [[:en:Season of the Inundation|Akhet]] Season'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-CAPTION = [[:en:Pyramid of Senusret II|Pyramid of Senusret II]] in [[:en:El Lahun|El-Lahun]] }} The Menchet Festival has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom of Egypt|qq=}}, during the reign of [[:en:Nyuserre Ini|Nyuserre Ini]]. It is directly related to the festival of {{ill|Day of the Sand Holes|de|Chenep-scha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day of the Sand Holes|qq=}}. == Background == === Old Kingdom === The Menchet Festival took place on the third day after the New Moon, following the Chenep-scha Festival, and symbolized the "clothing of the Moon." == Twelfth Dynasty == In honor of the deified {{ill|Senusret II|de|Sesostris II|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senusret II|qq=}}, during the reign of {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senusret III|qq=}}, the festival was also referred to as "The Clothing of Senusret II." It was celebrated after the "Day of Fetching Sand for Anubis on His Mountain" in the Pyramid City of Senusret II, the Blessed One. The [[:en:Pyramid of Senusret II|Pyramid of Senusret II]] at [[:en:El Lahun|El-Lahun]] was traditionally associated with the "Mountain of [[:en:Anubis|Anubis]]." In the papyri examined by [[:en:Ludwig Borchardt|Ludwig Borchardt]], festival dates are recorded for two different years: - 27th of Akhet II - 17th of Akhet II in the following year. These have since been correlated to the 36th and 37th regnal years of {{ill|Amenemhat III|de|Amenemhet III|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenemhat III|qq=}}. However, the similarly named month {{ill|Menchet (month)|de|Menchet (Monat)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (month)|qq=}} had no direct connection to this festival. The month was named after the goddess {{ill|Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|de|Menchet (ägyptische Mythologie)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|qq=}}, who was responsible for clothing the gods on their birthdays. == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}} |title=Sothic and Lunar Dates: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt |series=Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge |volume=20 |publisher=Gerstenberg |location=Hildesheim |year=1985 |isbn=3-8067-8086-X}} * {{Cite book |author=[[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |year=1950}} * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}} |editor=[[:en:Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|Academy of Sciences and Literature]] |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |year=1950}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} [[Category:pages that had Egyptian Calendar Navbar]] {{translated page|de|Menchet-Fest|version=201349399|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Religious Festivals of Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Festival Calendar of Month Akhet II]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Menchet Festival]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[sn:Menchet Festival]] [[de:Menchet-Fest]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}}

However, the similarly named month {{ill|Menchet (month)|de|Menchet (Monat)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (month)|qq=}} had no direct connection to this festival. The month was named after the goddess {{ill|Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|de|Menchet (ägyptische Mythologie)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|qq=}}, who was responsible for clothing the gods on their birthdays. == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}} |title=Sothic and Lunar Dates: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt |series=Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge |volume=20 |publisher=Gerstenberg |location=Hildesheim |year=1985 |isbn=3-8067-8086-X}} * {{Cite book |author=[[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |year=1950}} * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}} |editor=[[:en:Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|Academy of Sciences and Literature]] |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |year=1950}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} [[Category:pages that had Egyptian Calendar Navbar]] {{translated page|de|Menchet-Fest|version=201349399|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Religious Festivals of Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Festival Calendar of Month Akhet II]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Menchet Festival]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[sn:Menchet Festival]] [[de:Menchet-Fest]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Festkalender des Monats Achet II]]

However, the similarly named month {{ill|Menchet (month)|de|Menchet (Monat)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (month)|qq=}} had no direct connection to this festival. The month was named after the goddess {{ill|Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|de|Menchet (ägyptische Mythologie)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|qq=}}, who was responsible for clothing the gods on their birthdays. == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}} |title=Sothic and Lunar Dates: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt |series=Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge |volume=20 |publisher=Gerstenberg |location=Hildesheim |year=1985 |isbn=3-8067-8086-X}} * {{Cite book |author=[[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |year=1950}} * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}} |editor=[[:en:Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|Academy of Sciences and Literature]] |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |year=1950}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} [[Category:pages that had Egyptian Calendar Navbar]] {{translated page|de|Menchet-Fest|version=201349399|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Menchet Festival]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[sn:Menchet Festival]] [[de:Menchet-Fest]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Festival Calendar of the Month Achet II]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]]

The month was named after the goddess {{ill|Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|de|Menchet (ägyptische Mythologie)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|qq=}}, who was responsible for clothing the gods on their birthdays. == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}} |title=Sothic and Lunar Dates: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt |series=Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge |volume=20 |publisher=Gerstenberg |location=Hildesheim |year=1985 |isbn=3-8067-8086-X}} * {{Cite book |author=[[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |year=1950}} * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}} |editor=[[:en:Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|Academy of Sciences and Literature]] |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |year=1950}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Menchet-Fest|version=201349399|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Menchet Festival]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[sn:Menchet Festival]] [[de:Menchet-Fest]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Festival Calendar of the Month Achet II]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[sn:Menchet Festival]]

''ḥb=f-n-mnḫt-S(j)-n-Wsrt''
''Festival of the Clothing of {{ill|Senusret II|de|Sesostris II|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senusret II|qq=}}'' |NAME4-EXTENDED = '''Festival Date''' |NAME4 = N12:Z1*Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME4-TRANSLITERATION =''3ḫt'' |NAME4-EXPLANATION = ''Third Day of the Lunar Month in the Second Month of the [[:en:Season of the Inundation|Akhet]] Season'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-CAPTION = [[:en:Pyramid of Senusret II|Pyramid of Senusret II]] in [[:en:El Lahun|El-Lahun]] }} The Menchet Festival has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom of Egypt|qq=}}, during the reign of [[:en:Nyuserre Ini|Nyuserre Ini]]. It is directly related to the festival of {{ill|Sand-gathering Day|de|Chenep-scha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sand-gathering Day|qq=}}. == Background == === Old Kingdom === The Menchet Festival took place on the third day after the New Moon, following the Chenep-scha Festival, and symbolized the "clothing of the Moon." == Twelfth Dynasty == In honor of the deified {{ill|Senusret II|de|Sesostris II|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senusret II|qq=}}, during the reign of {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senusret III|qq=}}, the festival was also referred to as "The Clothing of Senusret II." It was celebrated after the "Day of Fetching Sand for Anubis on His Mountain" in the Pyramid City of Senusret II, the Blessed One. The [[:en:Pyramid of Senusret II|Pyramid of Senusret II]] at [[:en:El Lahun|El-Lahun]] was traditionally associated with the "Mountain of [[:en:Anubis|Anubis]]." In the papyri examined by [[:en:Ludwig Borchardt|Ludwig Borchardt]], festival dates are recorded for two different years: - 27th of Akhet II - 17th of Akhet II in the following year. These have since been correlated to the 36th and 37th regnal years of {{ill|Amenemhat III|de|Amenemhet III|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenemhat III|qq=}}. However, the similarly named month {{ill|Menchet (month)|de|Menchet (Monat)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (month)|qq=}} had no direct connection to this festival. The month was named after the goddess {{ill|Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|de|Menchet (ägyptische Mythologie)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|qq=}}, who was responsible for clothing the gods on their birthdays. == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}} |title=Sothic and Lunar Dates: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt |series=Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge |volume=20 |publisher=Gerstenberg |location=Hildesheim |year=1985 |isbn=3-8067-8086-X}} * {{Cite book |author=[[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |year=1950}} * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}} |editor=[[:en:Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|Academy of Sciences and Literature]] |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |year=1950}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Menchet-Fest|version=201349399|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Menchet Festival]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[sn:Menchet Festival]] [[de:Menchet-Fest]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Festival Calendar of the Month Achet II]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[sn:Menchet Festival]] [[Category:Missing wikidata]]

The month was named after the goddess {{ill|Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|de|Menchet (ägyptische Mythologie)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|qq=}}, who was responsible for clothing the gods on their birthdays. == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}} |title=Sothic and Lunar Dates: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt |series=Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge |volume=20 |publisher=Gerstenberg |location=Hildesheim |year=1985 |isbn=3-8067-8086-X}} * {{Cite book |author=[[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |year=1950}} * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}} |editor=[[:en:Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|Academy of Sciences and Literature]] |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |year=1950}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Menchet-Fest|version=201349399|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[de:Menchet-Fest]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Festival Calendar of the Month Achet II]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] {{wikidata link|Q1920275}}

Heb-ef-en-menchet-em-dju-Anubis
''ḥb=f-n-mnḫt-m-ḏw-Jnp.w''
''Festival of the Clothing of Anubis on His Mountain'' |NAME-EXTENDED = {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom of Egypt|qq=}} |NAME2-EXTENDED = [[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]] |NAME2 = W3:I9-N35-Y5:N35 Aa1:X1-F12-S29-D21:X1-O34:N35
Heb-ef-en-menchet-Senwosret
''ḥb=f-n-mnḫt-S(j)-n-Wsrt''
''Festival of the Clothing of {{ill|Senusret II|de|Sesostris II|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senusret II|qq=}}'' |NAME4-EXTENDED = '''Festival Date''' |NAME4 = N12:Z1*Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME4-TRANSLITERATION =''3ḫt'' |NAME4-EXPLANATION = ''Third Day of the Lunar Month in the Second Month of the [[:en:Season of the Inundation|Akhet]] Season'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-CAPTION = [[:en:Pyramid of Senusret II|Pyramid of Senusret II]] in [[:en:El Lahun|El-Lahun]] }} The Menchet Festival has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom of Egypt|qq=}}, during the reign of [[:en:Nyuserre Ini|Nyuserre Ini]]. It is directly related to the festival of {{ill|Sand-gathering Day|de|Chenep-scha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sand-gathering Day|qq=}}. == Background == === Old Kingdom === The Menchet Festival took place on the third day after the New Moon, following the Chenep-scha Festival, and symbolized the "clothing of the Moon." == Twelfth Dynasty == In honor of the deified {{ill|Senusret II|de|Sesostris II|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senusret II|qq=}}, during the reign of {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senusret III|qq=}}, the festival was also referred to as "The Clothing of Senusret II." It was celebrated after the "Day of Fetching Sand for Anubis on His Mountain" in the Pyramid City of Senusret II, the Blessed One. The [[:en:Pyramid of Senusret II|Pyramid of Senusret II]] at [[:en:El Lahun|El-Lahun]] was traditionally associated with the "Mountain of [[:en:Anubis|Anubis]]." In the papyri examined by [[:en:Ludwig Borchardt|Ludwig Borchardt]], festival dates are recorded for two different years: - 27th of Akhet II - 17th of Akhet II in the following year. These have since been correlated to the 36th and 37th regnal years of {{ill|Amenemhat III|de|Amenemhet III|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenemhat III|qq=}}. However, the similarly named month {{ill|Menchet (month)|de|Menchet (Monat)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (month)|qq=}} had no direct connection to this festival. The month was named after the goddess {{ill|Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|de|Menchet (ägyptische Mythologie)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|qq=}}, who was responsible for clothing the gods on their birthdays. == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}} |title=Sothic and Lunar Dates: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt |series=Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge |volume=20 |publisher=Gerstenberg |location=Hildesheim |year=1985 |isbn=3-8067-8086-X}} * {{Cite book |author=[[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |year=1950}} * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}} |editor=[[:en:Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|Academy of Sciences and Literature]] |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |year=1950}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Menchet-Fest|version=201349399|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[de:Menchet-Fest]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Festival Calendar of the Month Achet II]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] {{wikidata link|Q1920275}}

The month was named after the goddess {{ill|Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|de|Menchet (ägyptische Mythologie)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|comment=no wikipedias work}}, who was responsible for clothing the gods on their birthdays. == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|WD=Q91239}} |title=Sothic and Lunar Dates: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt |series=Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge |volume=20 |publisher=Gerstenberg |location=Hildesheim |year=1985 |isbn=3-8067-8086-X}} * {{Cite book |author=[[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |year=1950}} * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|WD=Q102228}} |editor=[[:en:Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|Academy of Sciences and Literature]] |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |year=1950}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Menchet-Fest|version=201349399|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[de:Menchet-Fest]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Festival Calendar of the Month Achet II]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] {{wikidata link|Q1920275}}

[[de:Menchet-Fest]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME = W3:I9-N35-Y5:N35 Aa1:X1-N26-G17-E16
Heb-ef-en-menchet-em-dju-Anubis
''ḥb=f-n-mnḫt-m-ḏw-Jnp.w''
''Festival of the Clothing of Anubis on His Mountain'' |NAME-EXTENDED = {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q177819}} |NAME2-EXTENDED = [[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]] |NAME2 = W3:I9-N35-Y5:N35 Aa1:X1-F12-S29-D21:X1-O34:N35
Heb-ef-en-menchet-Senwosret
''ḥb=f-n-mnḫt-S(j)-n-Wsrt''
''Festival of the Clothing of {{ill|Senusret II|WD=Q18722|1=Senusret II|2=en|3=Senusret II|lt=Senusret II|de|Sesostris II|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senusret II|comment=no wikipedias work}}'' |NAME4-EXTENDED = '''Festival Date''' |NAME4 = N12:Z1*Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME4-TRANSLITERATION =''3ḫt'' |NAME4-EXPLANATION = ''Third Day of the Lunar Month in the Second Month of the [[:en:Season of the Inundation|Akhet]] Season'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-CAPTION = [[:en:Pyramid of Senusret II|Pyramid of Senusret II]] in [[:en:El Lahun|El-Lahun]] }} The Menchet Festival has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q177819}}, during the reign of [[:en:Nyuserre Ini|Nyuserre Ini]]. It is directly related to the festival of {{ill|Sand-gathering Day|de|Chenep-scha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sand-gathering Day|WD=Q1069855}}. == Background == === Old Kingdom === The Menchet Festival took place on the third day after the New Moon, following the Chenep-scha Festival, and symbolized the "clothing of the Moon." == Twelfth Dynasty == In honor of the deified {{ill|Senusret II|WD=Q18722|1=Senusret II|2=en|3=Senusret II|lt=Senusret II|de|Sesostris II|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senusret II|comment=no wikipedias work}}, during the reign of {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senusret III|comment=no wikipedias work}}, the festival was also referred to as "The Clothing of Senusret II." It was celebrated after the "Day of Fetching Sand for Anubis on His Mountain" in the Pyramid City of Senusret II, the Blessed One. The [[:en:Pyramid of Senusret II|Pyramid of Senusret II]] at [[:en:El Lahun|El-Lahun]] was traditionally associated with the "Mountain of [[:en:Anubis|Anubis]]." In the papyri examined by [[:en:Ludwig Borchardt|Ludwig Borchardt]], festival dates are recorded for two different years: - 27th of Akhet II - 17th of Akhet II in the following year. These have since been correlated to the 36th and 37th regnal years of {{ill|Amenemhat III|de|Amenemhet III|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenemhat III|comment=no wikipedias work}}. However, the similarly named month {{ill|Menchet (month)|de|Menchet (Monat)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (month)|WD=Q1624441}} had no direct connection to this festival. The month was named after the goddess {{ill|Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|de|Menchet (ägyptische Mythologie)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|comment=no wikipedias work}}, who was responsible for clothing the gods on their birthdays. == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|WD=Q91239}} |title=Sothic and Lunar Dates: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt |series=Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge |volume=20 |publisher=Gerstenberg |location=Hildesheim |year=1985 |isbn=3-8067-8086-X}} * {{Cite book |author=[[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |year=1950}} * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|WD=Q102228}} |editor=[[:en:Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|Academy of Sciences and Literature]] |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |year=1950}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Menchet-Fest|version=201349399|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[de:Menchet-Fest]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Festival Calendar of the Month Achet II]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] {{wikidata link|Q1920275}}

It is directly related to the festival of {{ill|Sand-gathering Day|de|Chenep-scha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sand-gathering Day|WD=Q1069855}}. == Background == === Old Kingdom === The Menchet Festival took place on the third day after the New Moon, following the Chenep-scha Festival, and symbolized the "clothing of the Moon." == Twelfth Dynasty == In honor of the deified {{ill|Senusret II|WD=Q18722|1=Senusret II|2=en|3=Senusret II|lt=Senusret II|de|Sesostris II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senusret II|comment=no wikipedias work}}, during the reign of {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senusret III|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, the festival was also referred to as "The Clothing of Senusret II." It was celebrated after the "Day of Fetching Sand for Anubis on His Mountain" in the Pyramid City of Senusret II, the Blessed One. The [[:en:Pyramid of Senusret II|Pyramid of Senusret II]] at [[:en:El Lahun|El-Lahun]] was traditionally associated with the "Mountain of [[:en:Anubis|Anubis]]." In the papyri examined by [[:en:Ludwig Borchardt|Ludwig Borchardt]], festival dates are recorded for two different years: - 27th of Akhet II - 17th of Akhet II in the following year. These have since been correlated to the 36th and 37th regnal years of {{ill|Amenemhat III|de|Amenemhet III|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenemhat III|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}. However, the similarly named month {{ill|Menchet (month)|de|Menchet (Monat)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (month)|WD=Q1624441}} had no direct connection to this festival. The month was named after the goddess {{ill|Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|de|Menchet (ägyptische Mythologie)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, who was responsible for clothing the gods on their birthdays. == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|WD=Q91239}} |title=Sothic and Lunar Dates: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt |series=Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge |volume=20 |publisher=Gerstenberg |location=Hildesheim |year=1985 |isbn=3-8067-8086-X}} * {{Cite book |author=[[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |year=1950}} * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|WD=Q102228}} |editor=[[:en:Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|Academy of Sciences and Literature]] |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |year=1950}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Menchet-Fest|version=201349399|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[de:Menchet-Fest]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Festival Calendar of the Month Achet II]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] {{wikidata link|Q1920275}}
