[[de:Menchet-Fest]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME = W3:I9-N35-Y5:N35 Aa1:X1-N26-G17-E16
Heb-ef-en-menchet-em-dju-Anubis
''ḥb=f-n-mnḫt-m-ḏw-Jnp.w''
''Festival of the Clothing of Anubis on His Mountain'' |NAME-EXTENDED = {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q177819}} |NAME2-EXTENDED = [[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]] |NAME2 = W3:I9-N35-Y5:N35 Aa1:X1-F12-S29-D21:X1-O34:N35
Heb-ef-en-menchet-Senwosret
''ḥb=f-n-mnḫt-S(j)-n-Wsrt''
''Festival of the Clothing of {{ill|Senusret II|WD=Q18722|1=Senusret II|2=en|3=Senusret II|lt=Senusret II|de|Sesostris II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senusret II|comment=no wikipedias work}}'' |NAME4-EXTENDED = '''Festival Date''' |NAME4 = N12:Z1*Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME4-TRANSLITERATION =''3ḫt'' |NAME4-EXPLANATION = ''Third Day of the Lunar Month in the Second Month of the [[:en:Season of the Inundation|Akhet]] Season'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-CAPTION = [[:en:Pyramid of Senusret II|Pyramid of Senusret II]] in [[:en:El Lahun|El-Lahun]] }} The Menchet Festival has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q177819}}, during the reign of [[:en:Nyuserre Ini|Nyuserre Ini]]. It is directly related to the festival of {{ill|Sand-gathering Day|de|Chenep-scha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sand-gathering Day|WD=Q1069855}}. == Background == === Old Kingdom === The Menchet Festival took place on the third day after the New Moon, following the Chenep-scha Festival, and symbolized the "clothing of the Moon." == Twelfth Dynasty == In honor of the deified {{ill|Senusret II|WD=Q18722|1=Senusret II|2=en|3=Senusret II|lt=Senusret II|de|Sesostris II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senusret II|comment=no wikipedias work}}, during the reign of {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senusret III|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, the festival was also referred to as "The Clothing of Senusret II." It was celebrated after the "Day of Fetching Sand for Anubis on His Mountain" in the Pyramid City of Senusret II, the Blessed One. The [[:en:Pyramid of Senusret II|Pyramid of Senusret II]] at [[:en:El Lahun|El-Lahun]] was traditionally associated with the "Mountain of [[:en:Anubis|Anubis]]." In the papyri examined by [[:en:Ludwig Borchardt|Ludwig Borchardt]], festival dates are recorded for two different years: - 27th of Akhet II - 17th of Akhet II in the following year. These have since been correlated to the 36th and 37th regnal years of {{ill|Amenemhat III|de|Amenemhet III|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenemhat III|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}. However, the similarly named month {{ill|Menchet (month)|de|Menchet (Monat)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (month)|WD=Q1624441}} had no direct connection to this festival. The month was named after the goddess {{ill|Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|de|Menchet (ägyptische Mythologie)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, who was responsible for clothing the gods on their birthdays. == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|WD=Q91239}} |title=Sothic and Lunar Dates: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt |series=Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge |volume=20 |publisher=Gerstenberg |location=Hildesheim |year=1985 |isbn=3-8067-8086-X}} * {{Cite book |author=[[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |year=1950}} * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|WD=Q102228}} |editor=[[:en:Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|Academy of Sciences and Literature]] |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |year=1950}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} ''ḥb=f-n-mnḫt-m-ḏw-Jnp.w''
''Festival of the Clothing of Anubis on His Mountain'' |NAME-EXTENDED = {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q177819}} |NAME2-EXTENDED = [[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]] |NAME2 = W3:I9-N35-Y5:N35 Aa1:X1-F12-S29-D21:X1-O34:N35
Heb-ef-en-menchet-Senwosret
''ḥb=f-n-mnḫt-S(j)-n-Wsrt''
''Festival of the Clothing of {{ill|Senusret II|WD=Q18722|1=Senusret II|2=en|3=Senusret II|lt=Senusret II|de|Sesostris II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senusret II|comment=no wikipedias work}}'' |NAME4-EXTENDED = '''Festival Date''' |NAME4 = N12:Z1*Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME4-TRANSLITERATION =''3ḫt'' |NAME4-EXPLANATION = ''Third Day of the Lunar Month in the Second Month of the [[:en:Season of the Inundation|Akhet]] Season'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-CAPTION = [[:en:Pyramid of Senusret II|Pyramid of Senusret II]] in [[:en:El Lahun|El-Lahun]] }} The Menchet Festival has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q177819}}, during the reign of [[:en:Nyuserre Ini|Nyuserre Ini]]. It is directly related to the festival of {{ill|Sand-gathering Day|de|Chenep-scha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sand-gathering Day|WD=Q1069855}}. == Background == === Old Kingdom === The Menchet Festival took place on the third day after the New Moon, following the Chenep-scha Festival, and symbolized the "clothing of the Moon." == Twelfth Dynasty == In honor of the deified {{ill|Senusret II|WD=Q18722|1=Senusret II|2=en|3=Senusret II|lt=Senusret II|de|Sesostris II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senusret II|comment=no wikipedias work}}, during the reign of {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senusret III|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, the festival was also referred to as "The Clothing of Senusret II." It was celebrated after the "Day of Fetching Sand for Anubis on His Mountain" in the Pyramid City of Senusret II, the Blessed One. The [[:en:Pyramid of Senusret II|Pyramid of Senusret II]] at [[:en:El Lahun|El-Lahun]] was traditionally associated with the "Mountain of [[:en:Anubis|Anubis]]." In the papyri examined by [[:en:Ludwig Borchardt|Ludwig Borchardt]], festival dates are recorded for two different years: - 27th of Akhet II - 17th of Akhet II in the following year. These have since been correlated to the 36th and 37th regnal years of {{ill|Amenemhat III|de|Amenemhet III|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenemhat III|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}. However, the similarly named month {{ill|Menchet (month)|de|Menchet (Monat)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (month)|WD=Q1624441}} had no direct connection to this festival. The month was named after the goddess {{ill|Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|de|Menchet (ägyptische Mythologie)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, who was responsible for clothing the gods on their birthdays. == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|WD=Q91239}} |title=Sothic and Lunar Dates: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt |series=Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge |volume=20 |publisher=Gerstenberg |location=Hildesheim |year=1985 |isbn=3-8067-8086-X}} * {{Cite book |author=[[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |year=1950}} * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|WD=Q102228}} |editor=[[:en:Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|Academy of Sciences and Literature]] |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |year=1950}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} It is directly related to the festival of {{ill|Sand-gathering Day|de|Chenep-scha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sand-gathering Day|WD=Q1069855}}. == Background == === Old Kingdom === The Menchet Festival took place on the third day after the New Moon, following the Chenep-scha Festival, and symbolized the "clothing of the Moon." == Twelfth Dynasty == In honor of the deified {{ill|Senusret II|WD=Q18722|1=Senusret II|2=en|3=Senusret II|lt=Senusret II|de|Sesostris II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senusret II|comment=no wikipedias work}}, during the reign of {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senusret III|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, the festival was also referred to as "The Clothing of Senusret II." It was celebrated after the "Day of Fetching Sand for Anubis on His Mountain" in the Pyramid City of Senusret II, the Blessed One. The [[:en:Pyramid of Senusret II|Pyramid of Senusret II]] at [[:en:El Lahun|El-Lahun]] was traditionally associated with the "Mountain of [[:en:Anubis|Anubis]]." In the papyri examined by [[:en:Ludwig Borchardt|Ludwig Borchardt]], festival dates are recorded for two different years: - 27th of Akhet II - 17th of Akhet II in the following year. These have since been correlated to the 36th and 37th regnal years of {{ill|Amenemhat III|de|Amenemhet III|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenemhat III|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}. However, the similarly named month {{ill|Menchet (month)|de|Menchet (Monat)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (month)|WD=Q1624441}} had no direct connection to this festival. The month was named after the goddess {{ill|Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|de|Menchet (ägyptische Mythologie)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, who was responsible for clothing the gods on their birthdays. == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|WD=Q91239}} |title=Sothic and Lunar Dates: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt |series=Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge |volume=20 |publisher=Gerstenberg |location=Hildesheim |year=1985 |isbn=3-8067-8086-X}} * {{Cite book |author=[[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |year=1950}} * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|WD=Q102228}} |editor=[[:en:Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|Academy of Sciences and Literature]] |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |year=1950}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Menchet-Fest|version=201349399|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[de:Menchet-Fest]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[Category:Festival Calendar of the Month Achet II]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] {{wikidata link|Q1920275}}

The month was named after the goddess {{ill|Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|de|Menchet (ägyptische Mythologie)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, who was responsible for clothing the gods on their birthdays. == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|WD=Q91239}} |title=Sothic and Lunar Dates: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt |series=Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge |volume=20 |publisher=Gerstenberg |location=Hildesheim |year=1985 |isbn=3-8067-8086-X}} * {{Cite book |author=[[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |year=1950}} * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|WD=Q102228}} |editor=[[:en:Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|Academy of Sciences and Literature]] |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |year=1950}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Menchet-Fest|version=201349399|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| }} [[de:Menchet-Fest]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{wikidata link|Q1920275}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Festival Calendar of the Month Achet II]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

[[de:Menchet-Fest]] [[de:Menchet-Fest]] [[de:Menchet-Fest]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME = W3:I9-N35-Y5:N35 Aa1:X1-N26-G17-E16
Heb-ef-en-menchet-em-dju-Anubis
''ḥb=f-n-mnḫt-m-ḏw-Jnp.w''
''Festival of the Clothing of Anubis on His Mountain'' |NAME-EXTENDED = {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q177819}} |NAME2-EXTENDED = [[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]] |NAME2 = W3:I9-N35-Y5:N35 Aa1:X1-F12-S29-D21:X1-O34:N35
Heb-ef-en-menchet-Senwosret
''ḥb=f-n-mnḫt-S(j)-n-Wsrt''
''Festival of the Clothing of {{ill|Senusret II|WD=Q18722|1=Senusret II|2=en|3=Senusret II|lt=Senusret II|de|Sesostris II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senusret II|comment=no wikipedias work}}'' |NAME4-EXTENDED = '''Festival Date''' |NAME4 = N12:Z1*Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME4-TRANSLITERATION =''3ḫt'' |NAME4-EXPLANATION = ''Third Day of the Lunar Month in the Second Month of the [[:en:Season of the Inundation|Akhet]] Season'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-CAPTION = [[:en:Pyramid of Senusret II|Pyramid of Senusret II]] in [[:en:El Lahun|El-Lahun]] }} The Menchet Festival has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q177819}}, during the reign of [[:en:Nyuserre Ini|Nyuserre Ini]]. It is directly related to the festival of {{ill|Sand-gathering Day|de|Chenep-scha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sand-gathering Day|WD=Q1069855}}. == Background == === Old Kingdom === The Menchet Festival took place on the third day after the New Moon, following the Chenep-scha Festival, and symbolized the "clothing of the Moon." == Twelfth Dynasty == In honor of the deified {{ill|Senusret II|WD=Q18722|1=Senusret II|2=en|3=Senusret II|lt=Senusret II|de|Sesostris II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senusret II|comment=no wikipedias work}}, during the reign of {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senusret III|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, the festival was also referred to as "The Clothing of Senusret II." It was celebrated after the "Day of Fetching Sand for Anubis on His Mountain" in the Pyramid City of Senusret II, the Blessed One. The [[:en:Pyramid of Senusret II|Pyramid of Senusret II]] at [[:en:El Lahun|El-Lahun]] was traditionally associated with the "Mountain of [[:en:Anubis|Anubis]]." In the papyri examined by [[:en:Ludwig Borchardt|Ludwig Borchardt]], festival dates are recorded for two different years: - 27th of Akhet II - 17th of Akhet II in the following year. These have since been correlated to the 36th and 37th regnal years of {{ill|Amenemhat III|de|Amenemhet III|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenemhat III|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}. However, the similarly named month {{ill|Menchet (month)|de|Menchet (Monat)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (month)|WD=Q1624441}} had no direct connection to this festival. The month was named after the goddess {{ill|Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|de|Menchet (ägyptische Mythologie)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, who was responsible for clothing the gods on their birthdays. == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|WD=Q91239}} |title=Sothic and Lunar Dates: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt |series=Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge |volume=20 |publisher=Gerstenberg |location=Hildesheim |year=1985 |isbn=3-8067-8086-X}} * {{Cite book |author=[[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |year=1950}} * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|WD=Q102228}} |editor=[[:en:Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|Academy of Sciences and Literature]] |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |year=1950}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Menchet-Fest|version=201349399|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| }} [[de:Menchet-Fest]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{wikidata link|Q1920275}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Festival Calendar of the Month Achet II]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

''Festival of the Clothing of {{ill|Senusret II|WD=Q18722|1=Senusret II|2=en|3=Senusret II|lt=Senusret II|de|Sesostris II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senusret II|comment=no wikipedias work}}'' |NAME4-EXTENDED = '''Festival Date''' |NAME4 = N12:Z1*Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME4-TRANSLITERATION =''3ḫt'' |NAME4-EXPLANATION = ''Third Day of the Lunar Month in the Second Month of the {{ill|Season of the Inundation|en|Season of the Inundation|WD=Q770568|lt=Akhet}} Season'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-CAPTION = {{ill|Pyramid of Senusret II|en|Pyramid of Senusret II|WD=Q1698602|lt=Pyramid of Senusret II}} in {{ill|El Lahun|en|El Lahun|WD=Q56924|lt=El-Lahun}} }} The Menchet Festival has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q177819}}, during the reign of {{ill|Nyuserre Ini|en|Nyuserre Ini|WD=Q269200|lt=Nyuserre Ini}}. It is directly related to the festival of {{ill|Sand-gathering Day|de|Chenep-scha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sand-gathering Day|WD=Q1069855}}. == Background == === Old Kingdom === The Menchet Festival took place on the third day after the New Moon, following the Chenep-scha Festival, and symbolized the "clothing of the Moon." == Twelfth Dynasty == In honor of the deified {{ill|Senusret II|WD=Q18722|1=Senusret II|2=en|3=Senusret II|lt=Senusret II|de|Sesostris II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senusret II|comment=no wikipedias work}}, during the reign of {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senusret III|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, the festival was also referred to as "The Clothing of Senusret II." It was celebrated after the "Day of Fetching Sand for Anubis on His Mountain" in the Pyramid City of Senusret II, the Blessed One. The {{ill|Pyramid of Senusret II|en|Pyramid of Senusret II|WD=Q1698602|lt=Pyramid of Senusret II}} at {{ill|El Lahun|en|El Lahun|WD=Q56924|lt=El-Lahun}} was traditionally associated with the "Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|en|Anubis|WD=Q47534|lt=Anubis}}." In the papyri examined by {{ill|Ludwig Borchardt|en|Ludwig Borchardt|WD=Q60758|lt=Ludwig Borchardt}}, festival dates are recorded for two different years: - 27th of Akhet II - 17th of Akhet II in the following year. These have since been correlated to the 36th and 37th regnal years of {{ill|Amenemhat III|de|Amenemhet III|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenemhat III|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}. However, the similarly named month {{ill|Menchet (month)|de|Menchet (Monat)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (month)|WD=Q1624441}} had no direct connection to this festival. The month was named after the goddess {{ill|Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|de|Menchet (ägyptische Mythologie)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, who was responsible for clothing the gods on their birthdays. == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|WD=Q91239}} |title=Sothic and Lunar Dates: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt |series=Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge |volume=20 |publisher=Gerstenberg |location=Hildesheim |year=1985 |isbn=3-8067-8086-X}} * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|en|Richard Anthony Parker|WD=Q2148992|lt=Richard Anthony Parker}} |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |year=1950}} * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|WD=Q102228}} |editor={{ill|Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|en|Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|WD=Q414163|lt=Academy of Sciences and Literature}} |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |year=1950}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Menchet-Fest|version=201349399|comment=Imported full de history}} [[de:Menchet-Fest]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{wikidata link|Q1920275}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Festival Calendar of the Month Achet II]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

''Festival of the Clothing of {{ill|Senusret II|WD=Q18722|1=Senusret II|2=en|3=Senusret II|lt=Senusret II|de|Sesostris II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senusret II|comment=no wikipedias work}}'' |NAME4-EXTENDED = '''Festival Date''' |NAME4 = N12:Z1*Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME4-TRANSLITERATION =''3ḫt'' |NAME4-EXPLANATION = ''Third Day of the Lunar Month in the Second Month of the {{ill|Season of the Inundation|en|Season of the Inundation|WD=Q770568|lt=Akhet|1=Season of the Inundation}} Season'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-CAPTION = {{ill|Pyramid of Senusret II|en|Pyramid of Senusret II|WD=Q1698602|lt=Pyramid of Senusret II|1=Pyramid of Senusret II}} in {{ill|El Lahun|en|El Lahun|WD=Q56924|lt=El-Lahun|1=El Lahun}} }} The Menchet Festival has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q177819|1=Old Kingdom of Egypt}}, during the reign of {{ill|Nyuserre Ini|en|Nyuserre Ini|WD=Q269200|lt=Nyuserre Ini|1=Nyuserre Ini}}. It is directly related to the festival of {{ill|Sand-gathering Day|de|Chenep-scha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sand-gathering Day|WD=Q1069855|1=Sand-gathering Day}}. == Background == === Old Kingdom === The Menchet Festival took place on the third day after the New Moon, following the Chenep-scha Festival, and symbolized the "clothing of the Moon." == Twelfth Dynasty == In honor of the deified {{ill|Senusret II|WD=Q18722|1=Senusret II|2=en|3=Senusret II|lt=Senusret II|de|Sesostris II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senusret II|comment=no wikipedias work}}, during the reign of {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senusret III|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, the festival was also referred to as "The Clothing of Senusret II." It was celebrated after the "Day of Fetching Sand for Anubis on His Mountain" in the Pyramid City of Senusret II, the Blessed One. The {{ill|Pyramid of Senusret II|en|Pyramid of Senusret II|WD=Q1698602|lt=Pyramid of Senusret II|1=Pyramid of Senusret II}} at {{ill|El Lahun|en|El Lahun|WD=Q56924|lt=El-Lahun|1=El Lahun}} was traditionally associated with the "Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|en|Anubis|WD=Q47534|lt=Anubis|1=Anubis}}." In the papyri examined by {{ill|Ludwig Borchardt|en|Ludwig Borchardt|WD=Q60758|lt=Ludwig Borchardt|1=Ludwig Borchardt}}, festival dates are recorded for two different years: - 27th of Akhet II - 17th of Akhet II in the following year. These have since been correlated to the 36th and 37th regnal years of {{ill|Amenemhat III|de|Amenemhet III|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenemhat III|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}. However, the similarly named month {{ill|Menchet (month)|de|Menchet (Monat)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (month)|WD=Q1624441|1=Menchet (month)}} had no direct connection to this festival. The month was named after the goddess {{ill|Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|de|Menchet (ägyptische Mythologie)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (Egyptian mythology)|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, who was responsible for clothing the gods on their birthdays. == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|WD=Q91239|1=Rolf Krauss}} |title=Sothic and Lunar Dates: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt |series=Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge |volume=20 |publisher=Gerstenberg |location=Hildesheim |year=1985 |isbn=3-8067-8086-X}} * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|en|Richard Anthony Parker|WD=Q2148992|lt=Richard Anthony Parker|1=Richard Anthony Parker}} |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |year=1950}} * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|WD=Q102228|1=Siegfried Schott}} |editor={{ill|Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|en|Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|WD=Q414163|lt=Academy of Sciences and Literature|1=Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur}} |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |year=1950}} {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Menchet-Fest|version=201349399|comment=Imported full de history}} [[de:Menchet-Fest]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{wikidata link|Q1920275}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[Category:Festival Calendar of the Month Achet II]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

However, the similarly named month {{ill|Menchet (month)|de|Menchet (Monat)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (month)|qid=Q1624441|1=Menchet (month)}} had no direct connection to this festival.
